words.meaning_en: The method and practice of teaching as an academic subject or theoretical concept
words.example_sentence: Modern pedagogy emphasises critical thinking over rote memorisation.

one_word_sub.question: The art, science, and theory of teaching and education

words.sentence: The teacher training programme focused heavily on constructivist ______.

words.meaning_en: A person who is a member of an academy of arts or sciences; a highly learned scholar
words.example_sentence: The celebrated academician delivered the inaugural lecture at the conference.

one_word_sub.question: A distinguished scholar who is a member of an academy or institution of learning

words.sentence: The renowned ______ was invited to chair the national education policy committee.

words.meaning_en: The subjects comprising a course of study at a school or university
words.example_sentence: The school revised its curriculum to include coding and digital literacy.

one_word_sub.question: The complete set of courses and subjects offered at an educational institution

words.sentence: The new ______ was designed to bridge the gap between theory and practical skills.

words.meaning_en: A long essay or research paper on a particular subject, especially for a degree
words.example_sentence: She submitted her doctoral dissertation on ancient Sanskrit grammar.

one_word_sub.question: A long formal written thesis or research paper submitted for an academic degree

words.sentence: His ______ on rural water scarcity won him the national academic award.

words.meaning_en: A class or meeting for discussion or training, typically on an academic topic
words.example_sentence: The professor conducted a seminar on climate change policy.

one_word_sub.question: A small group discussion session led by an expert for academic or professional learning

words.sentence: Students were required to present their research findings at the departmental ______.

words.meaning_en: A grant awarded to support a student's education; also great academic learning
words.example_sentence: She received a scholarship to study at a prestigious foreign university.

one_word_sub.question: A financial grant given to a deserving student to support their education

words.sentence: His excellent performance earned him a full ______ to study engineering.

words.meaning_en: A person, especially a young one, with exceptional abilities or qualities
words.example_sentence: The young chess prodigy defeated the national champion at age twelve.

one_word_sub.question: A young person who has extraordinary and exceptional talent or ability

words.sentence: Recognised as a musical ______, he performed at Carnegie Hall at the age of ten.

words.meaning_en: Relating to or characteristic of the art or science of teaching
words.example_sentence: The pedagogical approach adopted by the school encouraged student participation.

one_word_sub.question: Relating to the theories and methods involved in education and teaching

words.sentence: The new ______ framework placed equal importance on creativity and academic rigour.

words.meaning_en: The world of universities and academic scholarship
words.example_sentence: She left academia to start her own technology company.

one_word_sub.question: The environment or world associated with universities, research, and scholarship

words.sentence: His ideas were too radical for ______ but found great success in the business world.

words.meaning_en: To make something clear by explaining it in detail
words.example_sentence: The professor elucidated the complex theorem with a simple diagram.

one_word_sub.question: To explain or clarify something complex so that it becomes easy to understand

words.sentence: Could you ______ the steps you followed to arrive at this conclusion?
